articles.title: Is Caleb Durbin’s turnaround enough to solidify his future with the Red Sox?
articles.description:
Over the last 30 days, Durbin has the best OPS on the Red Sox.

The post Is Caleb Durbin’s turnaround enough to solidify his future with the Red Sox? appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Caleb Durbin leads the Red Sox with the best OPS over the last 30 days.
His performance may influence his future with the team as the season progresses.
Boston residents could see increased excitement for the Red Sox if Durbin continues to excel.

articles.title: US striker Folarin Balogun says a yellow card, not red card, would have been ‘fair’ at the World Cup
articles.description:
“But, for me personally, I think a yellow card would have been fair. It’s something that’s happened, so we have to move forward and I have to accept it.”

The post US striker Folarin Balogun says a yellow card, not red card, would have been ‘fair’ at the World Cup appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: US striker Folarin Balogun discusses World Cup officiating decisions.
Balogun believes a yellow card would have been more appropriate than a red.
His comments reflect ongoing discussions about officiating standards affecting local soccer fans.

articles.title: Red Sox’ Aroldis Chapman breaks MLB relief pitcher strikeout record in save vs. Angels
articles.description:
Chapman passed Hoyt Wilhelm for the most all-time strikeouts as a reliever with his 1,364th on Friday night.

The post Red Sox’ Aroldis Chapman breaks MLB relief pitcher strikeout record in save vs. Angels appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Aroldis Chapman set a new MLB record for relief pitcher strikeouts on July 4.
Chapman achieved his 1,364th strikeout, surpassing Hoyt Wilhelm's long-standing record.
This milestone boosts Red Sox pride and enhances local fan engagement in Boston.

articles.title: Podcast series tells full story of Mark ‘The Bird’ Fidrych, Northborough’s own who shot to national stardom 50 years ago
articles.description:
Fidrych captured the nation’s fancy in 1976 with quirks that were both endearing and authentic.

The post Podcast series tells full story of Mark ‘The Bird’ Fidrych, Northborough’s own who shot to national stardom 50 years ago appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Podcast series explores Mark 'The Bird' Fidrych's rise to fame in 1976.
Fidrych, from Northborough, captivated fans with his unique playing style and personality.
Local residents celebrate Fidrych's legacy as a cultural icon in Massachusetts.
